description: NSAIDs like Ibuprofen may reduce the antihypertensive effect of ACE inhibitors like Lisinopril and increase the risk of kidney damage.
Concurrent use may cause excessive low blood pressure (hypotension). Monitor blood pressure closely.
Macrolide antibiotics like Azithromycin can increase the blood levels of statins, increasing the risk of muscle pain or rhabdomyolysis.
Proton Pump Inhibitors (PPIs) like Omeprazole can reduce the absorption of Levothyroxine. Take Levothyroxine at least 4 hours apart.
Combining SSRIs (Sertraline) with NSAIDs (Ibuprofen) increases the risk of gastrointestinal bleeding.
Thiazide diuretics can increase blood glucose levels, potentially countering the effect of Metformin. Blood sugar monitoring recommended.
Amlodipine can significantly increase exposure to Simvastatin. Limit Simvastatin dose to 20mg if taking Amlodipine.
Concomitant use increases the systemic exposure of Hydrocodone and risk of respiratory depression.
Beta-blockers can inhibit the bronchodilator effect of Albuterol and may precipitate bronchospasm in asthma patients.
